verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem implesset omnia verba sua in aures plebis, intravit Capharnaum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπει δὲ ἐπλήρωσεν πάντα τὰ ῥήματα αὐτοῦ εἰς τὰς ἀκοὰς τοῦ λαοῦ εἰσῆλθεν εἰς καπερναούμ
verses.king_james: Now when he had ended all his sayings in the audience of the people, he entered into Capernaum.

verses.latin_vulgate: Centurionis autem cujusdam servus male habens, erat moriturus: qui illi erat pretiosus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑκατοντάρχου δέ τινος δοῦλος κακῶς ἔχων ἤμελλεν τελευτᾶν ὃς ἦν αὐτῷ ἔντιμος
verses.king_james: And a certain centurion's servant, who was dear unto him, was sick, and ready to die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum audisset de Jesu, misit ad eum seniores Judæorum, rogans eum ut veniret et salvaret servum ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ περὶ τοῦ ἰησοῦ ἀπέστειλεν πρὸς αὐτὸν πρεσβυτέρους τῶν ἰουδαίων ἐρωτῶν αὐτὸν ὅπως ἐλθὼν διασώσῃ τὸν δοῦλον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when he heard of Jesus, he sent unto him the elders of the Jews, beseeching him that he would come and heal his servant.

verses.latin_vulgate: At illi cum venissent ad Jesum, rogabant eum sollicite, dicentes ei: Quia dignus est ut hoc illi præstes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ παραγενόμενοι πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν παρεκάλουν αὐτὸν σπουδαίως λέγοντες ὅτι ἄξιός ἐστιν ᾧ παρέξει τοῦτο
verses.king_james: And when they came to Jesus, they besought him instantly, saying, That he was worthy for whom he should do this:

verses.latin_vulgate: diligit enim gentem nostram, et synagogam ipse ædificavit nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπᾷ γὰρ τὸ ἔθνος ἡμῶν καὶ τὴν συναγωγὴν αὐτὸς ᾠκοδόμησεν ἡμῖν
verses.king_james: For he loveth our nation, and he hath built us a synagogue.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jesus autem ibat cum illis. Et cum jam non longe esset a domo, misit ad eum centurio amicos, dicens: Domine, noli vexari: non enim sum dignus ut sub tectum meum intres:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἐπορεύετο σὺν αὐτοῖς ἤδη δὲ αὐτοῦ οὐ μακρὰν ἀπέχοντος ἀπὸ τῆς οἰκίας ἔπεμψεν πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ ἑκατόνταρχος φίλους λέγων αὐτῷ, κύριε μὴ σκύλλου οὐ γὰρ εἰμι ἱκανός ἵνα ὑπὸ τὴν στέγην μου εἰσέλθῃς
verses.king_james: Then Jesus went with them. And when he was now not far from the house, the centurion sent friends to him, saying unto him, Lord, trouble not thyself: for I am not worthy that thou shouldest enter under my roof:

verses.latin_vulgate: propter quod et meipsum non sum dignum arbitratus ut venirem ad te: sed dic verbo, et sanabitur puer meus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ οὐδὲ ἐμαυτὸν ἠξίωσα πρὸς σὲ ἐλθεῖν ἀλλὰ εἰπὲ λόγῳ καὶ ἰαθήσεται ὁ παῖς μου
verses.king_james: Wherefore neither thought I myself worthy to come unto thee: but say in a word, and my servant shall be healed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et ego homo sum sub potestate constitutus, habens sub me milites: et dico huic, Vade, et vadit: et alii, Veni, et venit: et servo meo, Fac hoc, et facit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ ἐγὼ ἄνθρωπός εἰμι ὑπὸ ἐξουσίαν τασσόμενος ἔχων ὑπ᾽ ἐμαυτὸν στρατιώτας καὶ λέγω τούτῳ πορεύθητι καὶ πορεύεται καὶ ἄλλῳ ἕρχου καὶ ἔρχεται καὶ τῷ δούλῳ μου ποίησον τοῦτο καὶ ποιεῖ
verses.king_james: For I also am a man set under authority, having under me soldiers, and I say unto one, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo audito Jesus miratus est: et conversus sequentibus se turbis, dixit: Amen dico vobis, nec in Israël tantam fidem inveni.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ταῦτα ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐθαύμασεν αὐτόν καὶ στραφεὶς τῷ ἀκολουθοῦντι αὐτῷ ὄχλῳ εἶπεν λέγω ὑμῖν οὐδὲ ἐν τῷ ἰσραὴλ τοσαύτην πίστιν εὗρον
verses.king_james: When Jesus heard these things, he marvelled at him, and turned him about, and said unto the people that followed him, I say unto you, I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et reversi, qui missi fuerant, domum, invenerunt servum, qui languerat, sanum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑποστρέψαντες οἱ πεμφθέντες εἰς τὸν οἶκον εὗρον τὸν ἀσθενοῦντα δοῦλον ὑγιαίνοντα
verses.king_james: And they that were sent, returning to the house, found the servant whole that had been sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est: deinceps ibat in civitatem quæ vocatur Naim: et ibant cum eo discipuli ejus et turba copiosa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν τῇ ἑξῆς ἐπορεύετο εἰς πόλιν καλουμένην ναΐν καὶ συνεπορεύοντο αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἱκανοὶ, καὶ ὄχλος πολύς
verses.king_james: And it came to pass the day after, that he went into a city called Nain; and many of his disciples went with him, and much people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem appropinquaret portæ civitatis, ecce defunctus efferebatur filius unicus matris suæ: et hæc vidua erat: et turba civitatis multa cum illa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἤγγισεν τῇ πύλῃ τῆς πόλεως καὶ ἰδού, ἐξεκομίζετο τεθνηκὼς υἱὸς μονογενὴς τῇ μητρὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ αὐτὴ ἦν χήρα καὶ ὄχλος τῆς πόλεως ἱκανὸς σὺν αὐτῇ
verses.king_james: Now when he came nigh to the gate of the city, behold, there was a dead man carried out, the only son of his mother, and she was a widow: and much people of the city was with her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam cum vidisset Dominus, misericordia motus super eam, dixit illi: Noli flere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδὼν αὐτὴν ὁ κύριος ἐσπλαγχνίσθη ἐπ᾽ αὐτῇ καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῇ μὴ κλαῖε
verses.king_james: And when the Lord saw her, he had compassion on her, and said unto her, Weep not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et accessit, et tetigit loculum. (Hi autem qui portabant, steterunt.) Et ait: Adolescens, tibi dico, surge.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ προσελθὼν ἥψατο τῆς σοροῦ οἱ δὲ βαστάζοντες ἔστησαν καὶ εἶπεν νεανίσκε σοὶ λέγω ἐγέρθητι
verses.king_james: And he came and touched the bier: and they that bare *him* stood still. And he said, Young man, I say unto thee, Arise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et resedit qui erat mortuus, et cœpit loqui. Et dedit illum matri suæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀνεκάθισεν ὁ νεκρὸς καὶ ἤρξατο λαλεῖν καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτὸν τῇ μητρὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he that was dead sat up, and began to speak. And he delivered him to his mother.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accepit autem omnes timor: et magnificabant Deum, dicentes: Quia propheta magnus surrexit in nobis: et quia Deus visitavit plebem suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλαβεν δὲ φόβος ἅπαντας, καὶ ἐδόξαζον τὸν θεὸν λέγοντες ὅτι προφήτης μέγας ἐγήγερται ἐν ἡμῖν καὶ ὅτι ἐπεσκέψατο ὁ θεὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And there came a fear on all: and they glorified God, saying, That a great prophet is risen up among us; and, That God hath visited his people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exiit hic sermo in universam Judæam de eo, et in omnem circa regionem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ὁ λόγος οὗτος ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ ἰουδαίᾳ περὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐν πάσῃ τῇ περιχώρῳ
verses.king_james: And this rumour of him went forth throughout all Judaea, and throughout all the region round about.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nuntiaverunt Joanni discipuli ejus de omnibus his.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπήγγειλαν ἰωάννῃ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ περὶ πάντων τούτων
verses.king_james: And the disciples of John shewed him of all these things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et convocavit duos de discipulis suis Joannes, et misit ad Jesum, dicens: Tu es qui venturus es, an alium exspectamus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καί προσκαλεσάμενος δύο τινάς τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ ὁ ἰωάννης ἔπεμψεν πρὸς τὸν ἰησοῦν, λέγων, σὺ εἶ ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἢ ἄλλον προσδοκῶμεν
verses.king_james: And John calling *unto him* two of his disciples sent *them* to Jesus, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venissent ad eum viri, dixerunt: Joannes Baptista misit nos ad te dicens: Tu es qui venturus es, an alium exspectamus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραγενόμενοι δὲ πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ ἄνδρες εἶπον, ἰωάννης ὁ βαπτιστὴς ἀπέσταλκεν ἡμᾶς πρὸς σὲ λέγων, σὺ εἶ ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἢ ἄλλον προσδοκῶμεν
verses.king_james: When the men were come unto him, they said, John Baptist hath sent us unto thee, saying, Art thou he that should come? or look we for another?

verses.latin_vulgate: (In ipsa autem hora multos curavit a languoribus, et plagis, et spiritibus malis, et cæcis multis donavit visum.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν αὐτῇ δὲ τῇ ὥρᾳ ἐθεράπευσεν πολλοὺς ἀπὸ νόσων καὶ μαστίγων καὶ πνευμάτων πονηρῶν καὶ τυφλοῖς πολλοῖς ἐχαρίσατο τὸ βλέπειν
verses.king_james: And in that same hour he cured many of *their* infirmities and plagues, and of evil spirits; and unto many *that were* blind he gave sight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens, dixit illis: Euntes renuntiate Joanni quæ audistis et vidistis: quia cæci vident, claudi ambulant, leprosi mundantur, surdi audiunt, mortui resurgunt, pauperes evangelizantur:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς πορευθέντες ἀπαγγείλατε ἰωάννῃ ἃ εἴδετε καὶ ἠκούσατε ὅτι τυφλοὶ ἀναβλέπουσιν χωλοὶ περιπατοῦσιν λεπροὶ καθαρίζονται κωφοὶ ἀκούουσιν νεκροὶ ἐγείρονται πτωχοὶ εὐαγγελίζονται
verses.king_james: Then Jesus answering said unto them, Go your way, and tell John what things ye have seen and heard; how that the blind see, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, to the poor the gospel is preached.

verses.latin_vulgate: et beatus est quicumque non fuerit scandalizatus in me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μακάριός ἐστιν ὃς ἐὰν μὴ σκανδαλισθῇ ἐν ἐμοί
verses.king_james: And blessed is *he*, whosoever shall not be offended in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum discessissent nuntii Joannis, cœpit de Joanne dicere ad turbas: Quid existis in desertum videre? arundinem vento agitatam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπελθόντων δὲ τῶν ἀγγέλων ἰωάννου ἤρξατο λέγειν πρὸς τοὺς ὄχλους περὶ ἰωάννου τί ἐξεληλύθατε εἰς τὴν ἔρημον θεάσασθαι κάλαμον ὑπὸ ἀνέμου σαλευόμενον
verses.king_james: And when the messengers of John were departed, he began to speak unto the people concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness for to see? A reed shaken with the wind?

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed quid existis videre? hominem mollibus vestibus indutum? Ecce qui in veste pretiosa sunt et deliciis, in domibus regum sunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ τί ἐξεληλύθατε ἰδεῖν ἄνθρωπον ἐν μαλακοῖς ἱματίοις ἠμφιεσμένον ἰδού, οἱ ἐν ἱματισμῷ ἐνδόξῳ καὶ τρυφῇ ὑπάρχοντες ἐν τοῖς βασιλείοις εἰσίν
verses.king_james: But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? Behold, they which are gorgeously apparelled, and live delicately, are in kings' courts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed quid existis videre? prophetam? Utique dico vobis, et plus quam prophetam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ τί ἐξεληλύθατε ἰδεῖν προφήτην ναί λέγω ὑμῖν καὶ περισσότερον προφήτου
verses.king_james: But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? Yea, I say unto you, and much more than a prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: hic est, de quo scriptum est: Ecce mitto angelum meum ante faciem tuam, qui præparabit viam tuam ante te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτός ἐστιν περὶ οὗ γέγραπται ἰδού, ἐγώ ἀποστέλλω τὸν ἄγγελόν μου πρὸ προσώπου σου ὃς κατασκευάσει τὴν ὁδόν σου ἔμπροσθέν σου
verses.king_james: This is *he*, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico enim vobis: major inter natos mulierum propheta Joanne Baptista nemo est: qui autem minor est in regno Dei, major est illo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω γὰρ ὑμῖν μείζων ἐν γεννητοῖς γυναικῶν προφήτης ἰωάννου τοῦ βαπτιστοῦ οὐδείς ἐστιν ὁ δὲ μικρότερος ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ μείζων αὐτοῦ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: For I say unto you, Among those that are born of women there is not a greater prophet than John the Baptist: but he that is least in the kingdom of God is greater than he.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et omnis populus audiens et publicani, justificaverunt Deum, baptizati baptismo Joannis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πᾶς ὁ λαὸς ἀκούσας καὶ οἱ τελῶναι ἐδικαίωσαν τὸν θεόν βαπτισθέντες τὸ βάπτισμα ἰωάννου
verses.king_james: And all the people that heard *him*, and the publicans, justified God, being baptized with the baptism of John.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæi autem et legisperiti consilium Dei spreverunt in semetipsos, non baptizati ab eo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ φαρισαῖοι καὶ οἱ νομικοὶ τὴν βουλὴν τοῦ θεοῦ ἠθέτησαν εἰς ἑαυτούς μὴ βαπτισθέντες ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: But the Pharisees and lawyers rejected the counsel of God against themselves, being not baptized of him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ait autem Dominus: Cui ergo similes dicam homines generationis hujus? et cui similes sunt?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ ὁ κύριος, τίνι οὖν ὁμοιώσω τοὺς ἀνθρώπους τῆς γενεᾶς ταύτης καὶ τίνι εἰσὶν ὅμοιοι
verses.king_james: And the Lord said, Whereunto then shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?

verses.latin_vulgate: Similes sunt pueris sedentibus in foro, et loquentibus ad invicem, et dicentibus: Cantavimus vobis tibiis, et non saltastis: lamentavimus, et non plorastis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅμοιοί εἰσιν παιδίοις τοῖς ἐν ἀγορᾷ καθημένοις καὶ προσφωνοῦσιν ἀλλήλοις καὶ λέγουσιν, ηὐλήσαμεν ὑμῖν καὶ οὐκ ὠρχήσασθε ἐθρηνήσαμεν ὑμῖν, καὶ οὐκ ἐκλαύσατε
verses.king_james: They are like unto children sitting in the marketplace, and calling one to another, and saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned to you, and ye have not wept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit enim Joannes Baptista, neque manducans panem, neque bibens vinum, et dicitis: Dæmonium habet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλήλυθεν γὰρ ἰωάννης ὁ βαπτιστὴς μήτε ἄρτον ἐσθίων μὴτὲ οἶνον πίνων καὶ λέγετε δαιμόνιον ἔχει
verses.king_james: For John the Baptist came neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and ye say, He hath a devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit Filius hominis manducans, et bibens, et dicitis: Ecce homo devorator, et bibens vinum, amicus publicanorum et peccatorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλήλυθεν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐσθίων καὶ πίνων καὶ λέγετε ἰδού, ἄνθρωπος φάγος καὶ οἰνοπότης τελωνῶν φίλος καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν
verses.king_james: The Son of man is come eating and drinking; and ye say, Behold a gluttonous man, and a winebibber, a friend of publicans and sinners!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et justificata est sapientia ab omnibus filiis suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐδικαιώθη ἡ σοφία ἀπὸ τῶν τέκνων αὐτῆς πάντων
verses.king_james: But wisdom is justified of all her children.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogabat autem illum quidam de pharisæis ut manducaret cum illo. Et ingressus domum pharisæi discubuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἠρώτα δέ τις αὐτὸν τῶν φαρισαίων ἵνα φάγῃ μετ᾽ αὐτοῦ καὶ εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὴν οἶκὶαν τοῦ φαρισαίου ἀνεκλίθη
verses.king_james: And one of the Pharisees desired him that he would eat with him. And he went into the Pharisee's house, and sat down to meat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce mulier, quæ erat in civitate peccatrix, ut cognovit quod accubuisset in domo pharisæi, attulit alabastrum unguenti:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού, γυνὴ ἐν τῇ πόλει ἥτις ἦν ἁμαρτωλός ἐπιγνοῦσα ὅτι ἀνάκειται ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ τοῦ φαρισαίου κομίσασα ἀλάβαστρον μύρου
verses.king_james: And, behold, a woman in the city, which was a sinner, when she knew that *Jesus* sat at meat in the Pharisee's house, brought an alabaster box of ointment,

verses.latin_vulgate: et stans retro secus pedes ejus, lacrimis cœpit rigare pedes ejus, et capillis capitis sui tergebat, et osculabatur pedes ejus, et unguento ungebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ στᾶσα παρὰ τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ ὀπίσω κλαίουσα ἤρξατο βρέχειν τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ τοῖς δάκρυσιν καὶ ταῖς θριξὶν τῆς κεφαλῆς αὐτῆς ἐξέμασσεν καὶ κατεφίλει τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ καὶ ἤλειφεν τῷ μύρῳ
verses.king_james: And stood at his feet behind *him* weeping, and began to wash his feet with tears, and did wipe *them* with the hairs of her head, and kissed his feet, and anointed *them* with the ointment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem pharisæus, qui vocaverat eum, ait intra se dicens: Hic si esset propheta, sciret utique quæ et qualis est mulier, quæ tangit eum: quia peccatrix est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ φαρισαῖος ὁ καλέσας αὐτὸν εἶπεν ἐν ἑαυτῷ λέγων, οὗτος εἰ ἦν προφήτης ἐγίνωσκεν ἂν τίς καὶ ποταπὴ ἡ γυνὴ ἥτις ἅπτεται αὐτοῦ ὅτι ἁμαρτωλός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Now when the Pharisee which had bidden him saw *it*, he spake within himself, saying, This man, if he were a prophet, would have known who and what manner of woman *this is* that toucheth him: for she is a sinner.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Jesus, dixit ad illum: Simon, habeo tibi aliquid dicere. At ille ait: Magister, dic.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπεν πρὸς αὐτόν σίμων ἔχω σοί τι εἰπεῖν ὁ δέ φησίν διδάσκαλε εἰπέ
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering said unto him, Simon, I have somewhat to say unto thee. And he saith, Master, say on.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo debitores erant cuidam fœneratori: unus debebat denarios quingentos, et alius quinquaginta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δύο χρεωφειλέται ἦσαν δανειστῇ τινι ὁ εἷς ὤφειλεν δηνάρια πεντακόσια ὁ δὲ ἕτερος πεντήκοντα
verses.king_james: There was a certain creditor which had two debtors: the one owed five hundred pence, and the other fifty.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non habentibus illis unde redderent, donavit utrisque. Quis ergo eum plus diligit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ἐχόντων δὲ αὐτῶν ἀποδοῦναι ἀμφοτέροις ἐχαρίσατο τίς οὖν αὐτῶν εἶπὲ, πλεῖον αὐτόν ἀγαπήσει
verses.king_james: And when they had nothing to pay, he frankly forgave them both. Tell me therefore, which of them will love him most?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens Simon dixit: Æstimo quia is cui plus donavit. At ille dixit: Recte judicasti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ σίμων εἶπεν ὑπολαμβάνω ὅτι ᾧ τὸ πλεῖον ἐχαρίσατο ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὀρθῶς ἔκρινας
verses.king_james: Simon answered and said, I suppose that *he*, to whom he forgave most. And he said unto him, Thou hast rightly judged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et conversus ad mulierem, dixit Simoni: Vides hanc mulierem? Intravi in domum tuam, aquam pedibus meis non dedisti: hæc autem lacrimis rigavit pedes meos, et capillis suis tersit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ στραφεὶς πρὸς τὴν γυναῖκα τῷ σίμωνι ἔφη βλέπεις ταύτην τὴν γυναῖκα εἰσῆλθόν σου εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν ὕδωρ ἐπὶ τοὺς πόδας μου οὐκ ἔδωκας αὕτη δὲ τοῖς δάκρυσιν ἔβρεξέν μου τοὺς πόδας καὶ ταῖς θριξὶν τὴς κεφαλῆς αὐτῆς ἐξέμαξεν
verses.king_james: And he turned to the woman, and said unto Simon, Seest thou this woman? I entered into thine house, thou gavest me no water for my feet: but she hath washed my feet with tears, and wiped *them* with the hairs of her head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Osculum mihi non dedisti: hæc autem ex quo intravit, non cessavit osculari pedes meos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: φίλημά μοι οὐκ ἔδωκας αὕτη δὲ ἀφ᾽ ἡς εἰσῆλθον οὐ διέλιπεν καταφιλοῦσά μου τοὺς πόδας
verses.king_james: Thou gavest me no kiss: but this woman since the time I came in hath not ceased to kiss my feet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oleo caput meum non unxisti: hæc autem unguento unxit pedes meos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλαίῳ τὴν κεφαλήν μου οὐκ ἤλειψας αὕτη δὲ μύρῳ ἤλειψεν μου τοὺς πόδας
verses.king_james: My head with oil thou didst not anoint: but this woman hath anointed my feet with ointment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod dico tibi: remittuntur ei peccata multa, quoniam dilexit multum. Cui autem minus dimittitur, minus diligit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗ χάριν λέγω σοι ἀφέωνται αἱ ἁμαρτίαι αὐτῆς αἱ πολλαί ὅτι ἠγάπησεν πολύ ᾧ δὲ ὀλίγον ἀφίεται ὀλίγον ἀγαπᾷ
verses.king_james: Wherefore I say unto thee, Her sins, which are many, are forgiven; for she loved much: but to whom little is forgiven, *the same* loveth little.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ad illam: Remittuntur tibi peccata.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ αὐτῇ ἀφέωνταί σου αἱ ἁμαρτίαι
verses.king_james: And he said unto her, Thy sins are forgiven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cœperunt qui simul accumbebant, dicere intra se: Quis est hic qui etiam peccata dimittit?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἤρξαντο οἱ συνανακείμενοι λέγειν ἐν ἑαυτοῖς τίς οὗτός ἐστιν ὃς καὶ ἁμαρτίας ἀφίησιν
verses.king_james: And they that sat at meat with him began to say within themselves, Who is this that forgiveth sins also?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem ad mulierem: Fides tua te salvam fecit: vade in pace.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν δὲ πρὸς τὴν γυναῖκα ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε πορεύου εἰς εἰρήνην
verses.king_james: And he said to the woman, Thy faith hath saved thee; go in peace.
